nodes.label: visual vitest project (third project in vitest.browser.config.ts; include glob **/*.visual.browser.test.ts)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` runs a `visual` project; `*.visual.browser.test.ts` files run ONLY there (packages/site project globs unchanged, proven by a negative-control file-name)

nodes.label: matcher defaults (browser.expect.toMatchScreenshot: pixelmatch comparatorOptions, screenshotOptions, resolveDiffPath)
nodes.accept: vitest.browser.config.ts sets comparator defaults (includeAA false, threshold 0.1, allowedMismatchedPixelRatio 0.01) and `npm run check` stays green (config typechecks against installed @vitest/browser 4.1.9 types)

nodes.label: baseline path policy (custom resolveScreenshotPath -> co-located __baselines__/<test-file>/<name>-<browser>-<platform>.png)
nodes.accept: a visual test run writes its reference under `**/__baselines__/<test-file>/<name>-chromium-darwin.png`, NOT under `__screenshots__/`

nodes.label: gitignore split (track __baselines__/, keep __screenshots__/ + diff attachments ignored)
nodes.accept: `git check-ignore` rejects a path under `__baselines__/` (trackable) and still matches paths under `__screenshots__/` and the vitest attachments dir

nodes.label: chromium-only pixel truth (visual project pinned to the chromium instance; WebKit keeps computed-style legs)
nodes.accept: a full `npm run test:browser` executes visual legs exactly once (chromium); WebKit executes zero visual legs (project instances pin, or skipIf(server.browser !== 'chromium') fallback — either mechanism, proven by run output)

nodes.label: darwin platform encoding (keep vitest's default -<platform> filename suffix; macOS-only baselines named honestly)
nodes.accept: committed baseline filenames carry `-darwin`; the ADR states the non-darwin behavior (missing-baseline failure, never a cross-platform garbage diff)

nodes.label: npm run test:visual (vitest run --config vitest.browser.config.ts --project visual)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:visual` runs only the visual project and exits 0 on a clean tree

nodes.label: npm run test:visual:update (same + --update; the ONLY path that rewrites an existing baseline)
nodes.accept: after a deliberate pixel change: plain `npm run test:visual` FAILS without touching the committed baseline; `npm run test:visual:update` rewrites it; the re-run is green

nodes.label: v1 pilot scope (the two forcing ADRs; NOT a fleet sweep)
ADR-0105 calendar pixel legs (the (a)-floor + wide-range gestalt as toMatchScreenshot legs)
nodes.accept: calendar `*.visual.browser.test.ts` legs realize ADR-0105 Acceptance (a)/(b) as pixel assertions; green on chromium with committed baselines

nodes.label: ADR-0106 truncate pixel legs (the clipped-single-line + as-toggle invariant as toMatchScreenshot legs)
nodes.accept: text-truncate `*.visual.browser.test.ts` legs realize the ADR-0106 rendered-box assertions as pixel assertions; green on chromium with committed baselines

nodes.label: ADR-0110 (intake: adopt vitest-native pixel-diff harness, scoped v1)
nodes.accept: `python adr_check.py 0110-*.md` exits 0; Status stays `proposed` for coordinator ratification

nodes.label: ADR README index row for 0110
nodes.accept: index row present; the site-adr-index lint gate (`npm test`) stays green

actions.label: capture a single element's pixels stably across runs (animations disabled, caret hidden, device scale)
compare a capture against a committed baseline within a bounded tolerance (AA ignored, YIQ threshold, mismatch ratio cap)
fail with an inspectable diff artifact on mismatch
create a baseline deliberately and never overwrite one silently (first run = create + fail-for-review; overwrite only under --update)
keep baselines committable and reviewable in git
keep failure debris (diffs, on-failure captures) out of git
restrict pixel truth to one engine while the other keeps computed-style coverage
encode the macOS-only baseline constraint honestly so a foreign machine fails loud, not wrong
make pixel cost opt-in per suite via a deliberate filename convention
run inside the standing browser gate AND standalone
prove the harness on the two ADR legs that forced the intake
record the decision and route future geometry-ADR acceptance language to the harness

edges.why: the build starts only after the intake ADR is ratified — the ADR is the spec the config seam implements
the index row cites the record it indexes
the --project visual filter the script uses requires the project to exist
the update script filters to the same project
the pilot file's *.visual glob is routed by the project's include
the pilot file's *.visual glob is routed by the project's include
the pilot asserts through the configured comparator/screenshot defaults
the pilot asserts through the configured comparator/screenshot defaults
the pilot's first run writes references at the tracked path the resolver pins
the pilot's first run writes references at the tracked path the resolver pins
committing the pilot's baselines requires the ignore split to land first
committing the pilot's baselines requires the ignore split to land first
the pilot legs run under the single-engine policy (no WebKit baselines may be generated)
the pilot legs run under the single-engine policy (no WebKit baselines may be generated)
